entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Cajanus cajan (L.) Millsp., the crop studied in the field studies

entities.name: KENYA
entities.description: The country where the field studies were conducted

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The environmental variable that varied across locations and affected plant development

entities.name: ALTITUDE
entities.description: The factor that caused temperature to decrease as it increased

entities.name: TIME TO FLOWERING
entities.description: The duration from sowing to flowering, denoted as f

entities.name: TIME TO MATURITY
entities.description: The duration from sowing to maturity, denoted as m

entities.name: DURATION BETWEEN FLOWERING AND MATURITY
entities.description: The duration between flowering and maturity, denoted as fm

entities.name: PIGEONPEA LINES
entities.description: 63 lines of pigeonpea developed in India used in the study

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the pigeonpea lines were developed

entities.name: DEVELOPMENT RATE EQUATIONS
entities.description: Equations used to describe the rates of development (1/f, 1/m, and 1/fm)

entities.name: OPTIMUM TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The temperature range that allows for the fastest time to flowering and shortest duration between flowering and maturity

entities.name: SUPRA-OPTIMAL TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Temperatures above the optimum range that result in slower development

entities.name: HIGH NIGHT TEMPERATURES
entities.description: Nighttime temperatures that contributed to the supra-optimal effect on development

entities.name: PLANT GROWTH
entities.description: The overall growth of the pigeonpea plants, which was retarded by cool temperatures and enhanced by warm temperatures

entities.name: KABETE
entities.description: A specific location in Kenya where cool temperatures were observed to retard plant growth
